name: Grilled Pork Tacos & Papaya Salsa
recipeIngredient: Papaya, peeled, seeded, cut into 1/2 inch cubes
Red chili, seeded and finely chopped
Pork boneless center loin roast, cut into 2 x 1/4 inch strips
recipeInstructions:
1. Cook pork in a 10-inch skillet over medium heat for about 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until no longer pink. Drain.
2. Stir in papaya and pineapple. Heat, stirring occasionally, until hot.
3. Preheat oven to 425°F.
4. Spoon about 1/4 cup of the pork mixture onto half of each tortilla. Top with about 2 tablespoons of cheese. Fold tortillas over the filling.
5. Arrange five filled tortillas in an ungreased jelly roll pan (15 x 10 x 1 inch). Brush with melted margarine.
6. Bake uncovered for about 10 minutes or until light golden brown. Repeat with remaining tacos.
7. Serve with Papaya Salsa. Makes 5 servings.
8. Salsa: Mix all ingredients. Cover and refrigerate about 30 minutes or until chilled. Makes 3 cups salsa.
